title: 9 Herbal Essential Oils to Help You Conquer Jet Lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for jet lag because it helps the body adjust to a new sleep schedule by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, allowing the body to recover from travel and get back to its natural rhythm.
Linalyl acetate is good for jet lag because it helps to reduce stress and anxiety that can be caused by changes in time zones, promoting a good night's sleep and adjusting the body's internal clock.

Beta-caryophyllene is good for jet lag because it has anti-inflammatory properties and helps regulate the body's internal clock by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oil, also known as English lavender oil, is a popular natural remedy for jet lag. This is because it helps regulate your body's internal clock and reduces stress levels caused by long-distance travel. The oil contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect on the mind and body. When you use Lavandula angustifolia essential oil, it can help you relax and fall asleep faster, which is essential when you're trying to adjust to a new time zone. Additionally, the oil's ability to reduce anxiety and promote a good night's sleep can help alleviate some of the symptoms associated with jet lag, such as fatigue, headaches, and irritability. Using Lavandula angustifolia essential oil in the evening can also help signal to your body that it's time to sleep, making it easier to adjust to your new time zone and wake up feeling refreshed.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oils that help with jet lag, start by obtaining dried chamomile flowers from a trusted supplier. Next, use a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or jojoba oil and mix it with the dried chamomile flowers in a clean glass jar. The general ratio is one part chamomile flowers to three parts carrier oil. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, to allow the chamomile to infuse its properties into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, store the Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use it as needed to help alleviate jet lag symptoms, such as insomnia and anxiety, by inhaling it or applying a few drops to your pulse points.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for jet lag because it helps the body adjust to new sleep-wake cycles by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, allowing people to recover from the physical and mental strain of traveling across time zones.
Valeranone is good for jet lag because it helps the body regulate its internal clock and sleep-wake cycle by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels.
Valerenal is good for jet lag because it helps the body adjust to a new sleep schedule by reducing stress and anxiety caused by traveling across different time zones.
Isovaleric acid is good for jet lag because it helps regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by traveling across different time zones.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oil, also known as valerian oil, has properties that help with jet lag. When traveling across time zones, our body's internal clock gets confused, making it hard to fall asleep or stay awake. Valerian oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to regulate our sleep-wake cycle. It reduces stress and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep at the right time. Valerian oil also helps to balance our body's hormones, which can become disrupted due to time zone changes. By inhaling valerian oil, our brain gets a signal to relax and prepare for sleep, making it easier to adjust to the new time zone. Additionally, valerian oil has a sedative effect, which helps to reduce insomnia and restlessness caused by jet lag.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oil that can help with jet lag, you'll need fresh or dried chamomile flowers and a carrier oil. First, harvest or purchase fresh chamomile flowers and dry them thoroughly to preserve their properties. Then, fill a clean glass jar with the dried flowers and cover it with a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil. The general ratio is to use one part dried flowers to three parts carrier oil. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil infuse into the flowers. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Your Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oil is now ready to use. To alleviate jet lag, apply a few drops of the oil to your skin after a long flight, inhale it gently, or add it to a warm bath for relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal essential oils for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Roscmarinic acid helps with jet lag because it reduces stress and anxiety by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps the body adjust to a new sleep schedule.
Luteolin is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock by reducing inflammation and promoting the production of melatonin, a hormone that controls sleep-wake cycles.
Apigenin is good for jet lag because it helps reduce inflammation and stress in the body, promoting a good night's sleep and regulating the body's internal clock to adjust to new time zones.
Quercetin is good for jet lag because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and promote better sleep quality, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.
Limonene is good for jet lag because it helps regulate the body's internal clock and promotes relaxation by reducing stress and anxiety caused by traveling across different time zones.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a natural essential oil that can help with jet lag. When traveling across different time zones, our bodies struggle to adjust to the new sleep schedule. This can cause fatigue, insomnia, and other problems. Melissa officinalis oil has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help regulate the body's internal clock. It contains chemicals like linalool and linalyl acetate that promote relaxation and reduce stress. When inhaled, the oil can help slow down the heart rate and lower blood pressure, making it easier to fall asleep. Additionally, Melissa officinalis oil has been shown to have anxiolytic properties, which can help alleviate anxiety and restlessness associated with jet lag. By using Melissa officinalis essential oil in a diffuser or applying it topically, travelers can help their bodies adjust to the new time zone more quickly and reduce the negative effects of jet lag.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oil that can help with jet lag, you will need dried chamomile flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil, and a clean glass jar. First, dry the chamomile flowers completely in a warm, dark place or use a food dehydrator. Once the flowers are dry, add them to the glass jar and fill it with the carrier oil. Make sure the flowers are completely covered by the oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the oil infuse into the flowers. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the chamomile flowers. The resulting Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oil can be used to promote relaxation and help the body adjust to new time zones, making it a useful remedy for jet lag.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for jet lag because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate the body's internal clock and reduce the negative effects of travel-related stress on the immune system.
Kaempferol is good for jet lag because it helps reduce inflammation and stress in the body, making it easier to adapt to new time zones and promote a restful night's sleep.

Isorhapontigenin is good for jet lag because it helps regulate the body's internal clock and reduce the disruption caused by travel across different time zones, thereby improving sleep quality and alleviating the symptoms of jet lag.
Tannins, also known as Chamomile, are good for jet lag because they have a calming effect on the body, helping to regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by traveling across time zones.
Tryptophan is good for jet lag because it helps to increase serotonin levels in the brain, which can improve sleep quality, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata essential oil, also known as passionflower oil, is a natural remedy for jet lag. It helps to calm the mind and body, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone. The oil has a sedative effect, promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which is often caused by jet lag. It also helps to regulate sleep patterns, allowing the body to adjust to the new schedule. Additionally, passionflower oil contains antioxidants that can help to reduce fatigue and improve mood, making it easier to cope with the challenges of traveling across time zones. By promoting relaxation and regulating sleep patterns, passionflower oil can help to alleviate the symptoms of jet lag, making it a useful tool for those who travel frequently.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oil for jet lag relief, start by harvesting fresh chamomile flowers from a healthy plant. Gently pick the flowers in the morning, when they're at their best. Next, dry the flowers by spreading them out on a paper bag or a clean cloth in a warm, dry, dark place. Keep them away from direct sunlight to preserve their potency. Allow the flowers to dry completely, which may take a few days to a week, depending on the environment. Once the flowers are dry, you can extract the essential oil from them using a steam distillation process or by using a solvent like jojoba oil or coconut oil. For steam distillation, you'll need a distillation apparatus and a heat source. Combine the dried flowers with water in a distillation apparatus and heat the mixture gently. The steam will carry the essential oil from the flowers into a separate container, where it will condense and collect. You can also use a solvent extraction method by mixing the dried flowers with a carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Let the mixture sit for a few weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, until the oil has extracted from the flowers. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids. The resulting Matricaria chamomilla essential oil can be used in aromatherapy to help alleviate jet lag symptoms, such as insomnia and fatigue.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal essential oils for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for jet lag because it helps regulate the body's internal clock and reduce inflammation caused by travel stress, leading to a faster recovery from jet lag symptoms.

Luteolin is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock by reducing inflammation, improving sleep quality, and promoting antioxidant activity, which can alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and digestive problems.
Apigenin is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock by promoting the production of melatonin, which is a hormone that controls sleep and wakefulness.
Quercetin is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress caused by travel, which can disrupt the normal sleep-wake cycle.
Caffeic acid, also known as chamomile, is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce stress caused by travel across time zones.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia dracunculus essential oil, also known as tarragon oil, is a natural remedy for jet lag. This is because it has properties that help regulate the body's internal clock. When traveling across different time zones, our body's circadian rhythm gets disrupted, leading to fatigue, insomnia, and other jet lag symptoms. Artemisia dracunculus oil has a calming effect on the mind and body, which can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by jet lag. It also has a balancing effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate our sleep-wake cycle. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate headaches and body aches that often accompany jet lag. By inhaling or applying Artemisia dracunculus oil, the body can start to adjust to the new time zone, promoting a smoother transition and reducing the effects of jet lag.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oil that may help with jet lag, start by gathering a few fresh or dried chamomile flowers. You can grow them in your garden or purchase them from a local health food store. Next, place the chamomile flowers in a clean glass jar, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Cover the jar with a lid, but not too tightly, to allow for air circulation. Then, place the jar in a warm, dark place for about two to three weeks, shaking the jar gently every few days. This process is called "infusion" and it helps to release the oil from the chamomile flowers. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids. You can then use this herbal essential oil in a diffuser or apply it to your skin (after diluting it with a carrier oil) to help alleviate jet lag symptoms such as fatigue, insomnia, and stress.
remedies.image_alt: Artemisia dracunculus herbal essential oils for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for jet lag because it helps reduce inflammation and stress in the body, which can disrupt sleep patterns, allowing for easier adaptation to new time zones.
Bilobalide is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce stress caused by travel, allowing for easier adjustment to a new time zone.

Kaempferol is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce stress caused by traveling across different time zones, allowing for a smoother transition to the new local time.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oil is believed to help with jet lag due to its ability to improve blood flow and oxygenation to the brain. When traveling across time zones, the body's internal clock can get disrupted, leading to feelings of fatigue, confusion, and disorientation. Ginkgo biloba oil helps to increase the flow of blood to the brain, which in turn can improve cognitive function and reduce feelings of tiredness. This can help people who are experiencing jet lag to feel more alert and focused, making it easier to adjust to their new time zone. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba oil has antioxidant properties that can help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can also be beneficial when dealing with jet lag. Some people also use Ginkgo biloba oil to help reduce stress and anxiety associated with traveling, which can further contribute to the challenges of jet lag.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oil that can help alleviate jet lag, start by harvesting the flowers of the chamomile plant, specifically the white or pale yellow varieties, as they are considered to be the most effective for relaxation and sleep. Gently dry the flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area, away from direct sunlight, to prevent spoilage. Once the flowers are completely dry, add them to a clean glass jar and cover them with a carrier oil such as jojoba or sweet almond oil in a 1:5 ratio (1 part dried flowers to 5 parts carrier oil). Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, to allow the oil to extract the medicinal properties from the chamomile flowers. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Finally, store the Matricaria chamomilla essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use it as needed, typically by adding a few drops to a diffuser, inhaling it directly, or mixing it with a carrier oil for a relaxing massage oil to help alleviate jet lag symptoms such as insomnia and fatigue.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol helps with jet lag because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce stress and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.
Shogaol is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce stress caused by traveling across different time zones.
Zingiberene is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce inflammation caused by travel fatigue.
Bisabolol is good for jet lag because it helps reduce stress and promotes relaxation, making it easier for the body to adjust to a new sleep schedule.
Curcumenol is good for jet lag because it helps regulate the body's internal clock and reduce inflammation caused by travel stress.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, or ginger essential oil, can help with jet lag due to its ability to reduce nausea and inflammation. When traveling across time zones, many people experience motion sickness, which can cause stomach problems and discomfort. Ginger oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea, making it easier to adjust to new time zones. Additionally, ginger oil has been shown to promote digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and bloating, common side effects of jet lag. It can also help to regulate the body's internal clock by promoting a sense of calm and relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep in a new time zone.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oils that help with jet lag, you will need fresh or dried chamomile flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil, and a clean glass bottle. Start by gathering or purchasing fresh chamomile flowers, and then dry them completely to preserve their potency. Next, fill a clean glass jar with the dried chamomile flowers, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Pour a carrier oil over the flowers, making sure they are completely covered, and then seal the jar. Place the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days, to allow the oil to infuse with the chamomile's healing properties. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, and discard the solids. This Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oil can be used in aromatherapy to help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue, stress, and insomnia, by promoting relaxation and calming the mind and body.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides are good for jet lag because they help reduce stress and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to new sleep patterns and wake up feeling refreshed.
Saponins (Chamomile) are good for jet lag because they have a calming effect on the body, helping to regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by traveling across different time zones.

Ferulic acid is good for jet lag because it can help reduce inflammation, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality, which can alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, headaches, and disrupted sleep patterns.

Phenolic acids are good for jet lag because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce stress and inflammation caused by long-distance travel, leading to a smoother and quicker recovery from jet lag symptoms.
Flavonoids are good for jet lag because they help regulate the body's internal clock and reduce symptoms of insomnia and fatigue that often occur when traveling across different time zones.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa essential oil is a natural remedy for jet lag. This oil comes from oats, which is a plant that grows in many parts of the world. When we travel across time zones, our bodies get confused and have trouble adjusting to the new time. Avena sativa essential oil helps to calm and soothe our bodies, making it easier to fall asleep and wake up at the right times. It also helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common problems when we travel. By promoting a good night's sleep, Avena sativa essential oil can help our bodies adjust to the new time zone faster. This oil can be used in aromatherapy, applied to the skin, or added to bath water to promote relaxation and reduce jet lag symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla, also known as German chamomile, herbal essential oil that helps with jet lag, you'll need dried chamomile flowers. Start by filling a clean glass jar with the dried flowers, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Next, cover the jar with a paper towel and place it in a warm, dark place for 2-3 weeks. This will allow the flowers to release their oils, which you'll collect in a separate container. After the 2-3 weeks, carefully strain the liquid through a coffee filter into a clean bottle, and discard the solids. You can also use a solvent like jojoba oil to help extract the oils from the flowers, but be sure to use a small amount and mix well to avoid any potential skin irritation. Once you've collected the oil, store it in a cool, dark place and use it to help calm your mind and body after traveling. To use the oil, simply add a few drops to a warm bath or inhale it directly from a handkerchief, and let the soothing effects of Matricaria chamomilla help you adjust to your new time zone.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal essential oils for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Apigenin is good for jet lag because it has been shown to help regulate the body's internal clock and reduce stress caused by travel, promoting a quicker recovery from jet lag.
Luteolin is good for jet lag because it helps regulate the body's internal clock and reduces inflammation caused by traveling across different time zones.
Quercetin is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate body clocks and reduce inflammation caused by travel, which can make it easier to adapt to a new time zone.
Chamazulene is good for jet lag because it helps to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can improve sleep quality and help the body adjust to a new time zone.
Kaempferol is good for jet lag because it helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can disrupt sleep patterns caused by traveling across different time zones.

remedies.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla essential oil is good for jet lag because it helps to calm the mind and body. When we travel to a new time zone, our bodies can get confused and have trouble sleeping at the right time. This oil has a soothing effect on our nervous system, which can help us relax and fall asleep faster. It also reduces stress and anxiety, making it easier to adjust to the new time zone. Additionally, Matricaria chamomilla oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce fatigue and promote a sense of well-being. When inhaled, it can also help to regulate our body's internal clock, making it easier to adapt to the new time zone. By using Matricaria chamomilla essential oil, we can help to minimize the negative effects of jet lag and feel more refreshed and energized.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oils that help with jet lag, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, you need to dry the flowers of the Matricaria chamomilla plant. This can be done by tying the stems together and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the flowers are completely dry, you can crush them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, you'll need a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil. Combine one part of the chamomilla powder with three parts of the carrier oil in a small bowl, and stir well to make a smooth paste. Store the mixture in a dark glass bottle and let it sit for two weeks, shaking the bottle every day. After two weeks, the mixture will have turned into a fragrant essential oil that can be used to help with jet lag. Simply inhale the oil or apply a few drops to your skin to feel more relaxed and energized.
remedies.image_alt: Matricaria chamomilla herbal essential oils for jet lag

intro_desc: Jet lag can cause problems like fatigue, headaches, and difficulty sleeping. This can affect your daily routine and make you feel irritable. Herbal essential oils can help alleviate these symptoms. Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender oil, is known for its calming properties. It can help you relax and prepare for sleep. Adding lavender oil to your bedtime routine can improve the quality of your sleep.  Valeriana officinalis, or valerian root oil, is also helpful in this situation. It's known to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which can be beneficial when dealing with jet lag. Using valerian root oil before bedtime can help you fall asleep faster and improve the quality of your sleep.  Fixing the issue of jet lag is crucial because it affects your daily life and well-being. When you're well-rested, you're more productive, and you can enjoy activities without feeling tired. This can also improve your mental health and reduce stress levels. Using herbal essential oils like lavender and valerian root can be a natural and effective way to alleviate jet lag symptoms and improve your quality of life.  To use lavender oil, you can add a few drops to your pillow or inhale it directly from the bottle. Valerian root oil can be added to your bath water or used in a diffuser. Start with a small amount and adjust as needed. It's also essential to maintain a consistent sleep schedule and create a relaxing sleep environment to help your body adjust to the new time zone.
supplementary_best: To remedy jet lag, a combination of herbal essential oils from 'Valeriana officinalis', 'Melissa officinalis', and 'Zingiber officinale' can be beneficial. 'Valeriana officinalis' oil, also known as valerian oil, has sedative properties that help with falling asleep and staying asleep. This is useful for people who experience insomnia or difficulty adjusting to a new time zone. 'Melissa officinalis' oil, also known as lemon balm oil, has a calming effect that can reduce stress and anxiety caused by jet lag. It also promotes relaxation and can help with falling asleep. 'Zingiber officinale' oil, also known as ginger oil, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help with nausea and stomach discomfort often associated with jet lag. This combination provides a balanced approach to addressing the physical and mental symptoms of jet lag, making it a suitable remedy.  When using this combination, it's recommended to blend a few drops of each oil and apply it to the pulse points or inhale it through steam inhalation. It's also essential to use high-quality, pure essential oils that are free from chemicals and additives. Using this combination may help alleviate the symptoms of jet lag and promote a smoother transition to a new time zone.  Note: As with any essential oil remedy, it's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional or a certified aromatherapist before use, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.
supplementary_avoid: If you have jet lag, there are some herbal essential oils you should avoid using. Peppermint oil (Mentha piperita) and eucalyptus oil (Eucalyptus globulus) can be too stimulating, which may interfere with your body's ability to adjust to a new time zone. They have a cooling and decongestant effect, but they can also make it harder for you to fall asleep when you need to. Another oil to avoid is rosemary oil (Rosmarinus officinalis), which can increase alertness and make it difficult to relax. This can make jet lag symptoms worse, especially if you're trying to fall asleep. Additionally, bergamot oil (Citrus aurantium bergamia) and geranium oil (Pelargonium graveolens) can be too intense and overwhelming, which may disrupt your sleep patterns. These oils can also be too stimulating for people who are already experiencing stress and fatigue from traveling.
supplementary_other_remedies: When traveling across time zones, jet lag can be a real challenge. In addition to using herbal essential oils, there are other natural remedies that can help. Magnesium-rich foods like dark leafy greens, nuts, and seeds can calm the body and mind, helping to regulate sleep patterns. This is because magnesium helps to relax the muscles, calm the nervous system, and regulate the body's internal clock. Drinking plenty of water is also essential, as dehydration can exacerbate jet lag symptoms. Staying hydrated helps to flush out toxins and keep the body's systems functioning properly, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone. Getting some morning sunlight exposure can also help to regulate the body's circadian rhythms, which is essential for feeling alert and awake during the day. Furthermore, practicing yoga or meditation can help to reduce stress and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep, even when traveling across time zones.  Another natural remedy that can help with jet lag is melatonin, a hormone that regulates sleep-wake cycles. Taking a melatonin supplement can help to induce sleepiness when it's time to sleep, even if the body is not yet adjusted to the new time zone. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before taking any supplements, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience jet lag often suffer from headaches, fatigue, and digestive problems. Medicinal herbs like feverfew and ginger can help with headaches. Feverfew contains a chemical that blocks pain-causing chemicals in the brain. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and inflammation.  Some people with jet lag may also experience insomnia or difficulty sleeping. The herb valerian root has a calming effect on the body, promoting relaxation and helping people fall asleep faster. It contains a chemical that slows down brain activity, making it easier to fall asleep.  Another common issue with jet lag is dehydration, which can lead to constipation. Dandelion root tea can help with digestion and relieve constipation. It contains a chemical that stimulates digestion and helps the body absorb nutrients.  People with jet lag may also experience digestive issues like nausea and bloating. Peppermint oil can help with these symptoms. It has a calming effect on the digestive system and reduces inflammation in the stomach.  Lastly, jet lag can cause fatigue and decreased productivity. Ashwagandha is an herb that can help with fatigue. It contains a chemical that helps the body adapt to stress and promotes energy and vitality.  These medicinal herbs work by targeting the underlying causes of the symptoms and helping the body adjust to the new environment and time zone.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for jet lag
